subject: refactor(dashboard): Phase 3 — revert @theme/@utility, use stock Tailwind
body:
Part of the vanilla-DaisyUI sweep. Removes every custom @theme token
and @utility directive from styles.css, restoring stock Tailwind
utilities at every JSX consumer.

Deleted from styles.css:
  @theme block:
    --text-8, --text-9, --text-10, --text-11, --text-13
    --shadow-tooltip, --shadow-dropdown, --shadow-dropzone-glow
  @utility directives:
    grid-heatmap-m, grid-heatmap-d
    w-modal-responsive, max-w-viewport-margin, min-h-hero
    text-shadow-primary-glow

JSX replacements:
  text-8/9/10/11 → text-xs (12px)
  text-13 → text-sm (14px)
  shadow-tooltip → shadow-lg
  shadow-dropdown → shadow-xl
  shadow-dropzone-glow → ring-2 ring-primary ring-offset-2
  text-shadow-primary-glow → font-semibold (no glow)
  grid-heatmap-m/d → flex + grid-cols-7 restructure
  w-modal-responsive → DaisyUI modal-box default
  max-w-viewport-margin → DaisyUI modal-box default
  min-h-hero → min-h-screen

Timing heatmap restructured:
  - Weekly heatmap: dropped per-cell count text (too small to read
    at 16px cells with 12px text). Tooltip still shows count.
  - Full heatmap: restructured from `grid-cols-[36px_repeat(7,1fr)]`
    to flex with fixed label column + inner grid-cols-7 for day cells
  - All cells drop inline text labels — tooltip-only count display

Tests: 61/61 pass. daisyui-surfaces.test.mjs TabBar Phase 7 assertion
updated from /text-shadow-primary-glow/ to /font-semibold/ to match
the new vanilla active-tab style.

Verified: vite build clean (3.43s), no arbitrary bracket values or
@theme/@utility references remain in dashboard/js.

Tags: refactor, ui, tailwind, daisyui, cleanup, vanilla
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-v5-migration
Semver: patch
title: Phase 3 — revert @theme/@utility, use stock Tailwind
